page: Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dsv7 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 128 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 132,083.9 and a PassMark CPU score of 123,481.9. The Intel Xeon processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions), contribute to a PassMark extended instructions score of 158,056.48. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching 9,470.27 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 452.95 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2,262.41.

This instance is highly capable for parallelized workloads, database operations, and static web serving, as demonstrated by a static web serving throughput of 179.77 GB/sec and 184.13 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. It also shows strong performance in LLM prompt processing, handling 245.57 tokens per second on a 7B model. However, the lack of local storage requires external network-attached storage, and the absence of dedicated GPUs limits its efficiency for heavy LLM text generation or deep learning training. It represents a high-density resource profile suitable for enterprise-grade general-purpose applications, multi-threaded compilation, and high-throughput web architectures.
description: The Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dsv7 family, powered by an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor. It provides 128 dedicated vCPUs across 64 physical cores, paired with 512.0 GB of RAM, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Running on Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 123,481.9 and exceptional memory bandwidth of up to 9,470.27 GB/sec for cached reads. It excels in highly parallelized tasks, showing strong performance in Redis operations and static web serving. The D128s_v7 is best suited for high-throughput web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 128 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 512 GB of RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D128s_v7 is a general-purpose VM with 128 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 512 GB RAM, optimized for high-throughput multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A high-density, 128-vCPU general-purpose instance delivering top-tier multi-core compute and exceptional memory bandwidth for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 128 dedicated vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6973P-C processors.
512.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core.
Top-tier multi-core performance and high-speed memory bandwidth.
Supports Premium Storage with no local storage bundled.
Excellent fit for high-throughput database operations and static web serving.
